title: Big Tech bans Trump, ramifications for the First Amendment & the open Internet
description:

Referenced in the show:

"I Have Blood on My Hands": A Whistleblower Says Facebook Ignored Global Political Manipulation

https://www.buzzfeednews.com/article/craigsilverman/facebook-ignore-political-manipulation-whistleblower-memo

Show Notes:

0:00 David Sacks intros the besties

2:08 Jason & Sacks hash it out & the besties break down reconciliation in American democracy

21:09 Big Tech bans, did they give Trump an easy out? Ramifications for First Amendment

43:01 What laws can be written to prevent Big Tech oligarchy in the future?

59:32 Why Big Tech acted in unison against Trump: internal & external pressure, pending Democratic administration

1:09:42 Current Pence/Trump relationship, McCarthyism 2.0, should Big Tech be broken up?

1:32:46 History of the presidential pardon, Chamath on SoFi's Anthony Noto

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses Big Tech's ban on Trump, its implications for the First Amendment, and potential laws to prevent Big Tech oligarchy. The hosts also explore the current relationship between Pence and Trump, and whether Big Tech should be broken up. They examine the history of presidential pardons and touch on SoFi's Anthony Noto.

takeaways.text: Big Tech's ban on Trump may have given him an easy out
New laws are needed to prevent Big Tech oligarchy
Big Tech acted in unison against Trump due to internal and external pressure
The current Pence/Trump relationship is strained
The hosts debate whether Big Tech should be broken up
The history of presidential pardons is complex
SoFi's Anthony Noto is a notable figure in the tech industry

sentiment.notes: The hosts express concern about the implications of Big Tech's ban on Trump and the potential for oligarchy. They also discuss the complexities of presidential pardons and the need for new laws to regulate Big Tech.

notable_moments.description: Discussion of Big Tech's ban on Trump
Debate on laws to prevent Big Tech oligarchy
Discussion of the current Pence/Trump relationship
